You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0005/sessions/0007-horizon-T5

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Recommended focus
Task: T5
Title: Extract & prove Pic⁰ IdentityComponent (FBC-independent picrep spine)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent*.lean

Advance the Picard-representability (picrep) spine WITHOUT depending on flat base change (FBC/02KH), so this runs fully in parallel with the live FBC run (T2) in AJC.

STEP 0 (gate — do this first, report before proceeding): Verify that the `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` import cone is genuinely FBC/cohomology-independent. Trace its transitive imports in MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge. If the cone pulls in the Čech/cohomology base-change engine (Cohomology/Cech*, the 02KH flat-base-change modules), STOP and report — do not extract; instead reduce scope to only the FBC-free declarations. If clean, continue.

STEP 1 (extraction — own build dir, MUST NOT build the AJC tree): Create a NEW standalone Lean subproject (e.g. SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent) with its own lakefile.toml (require mathlib rev v4.31.0, packagesDir ../../.lake-packages), lean-toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.31.0, and its own .lake — mirroring the Albanese/GR-Quot-Closure extraction pattern. Copy in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` and the minimal import cone needed to build it. Register it with `horizon project add` (enable vcs git_dir). The point of a separate subproject is build isolation: it must NOT touch or rebuild the AJC .lake that T2 is using.

STEP 2 (prove): Close the 9 structural sorries in IdentityComponent — Pic⁰ as the open-and-closed connected-component subgroup functor and the abelian-group-structure (Yoneda) transport — the parts that do NOT need the cohomology / flat-base-change engine. Leave any sorry that genuinely depends on FBC or full FGA representability documented and untouched, with a note on the blocker.

DELIVERABLE: the new subproject builds GREEN (lake build EXIT=0) with the FBC-free IdentityComponent sorries closed and axiom-clean; blueprint/DAG updated; it is positioned to merge back into AJC sorry-free like Albanese/GR. Reads from the AJC tree as source.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).

In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. - FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker` (note `P.ker` unfolds to `RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring S)` by rfl-level abbrevs); quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` — a Noetherian local ring with `finrank Cotangent ≤ ringKrullDim` is regular.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis inside later applications (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does — same idiom as Mathlib's `finite_of_algHom_finiteType_of_isJacobsonRing`).

**Remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`: Stacks 00OF** (localisation of regular local is regular) — needs Serre's finite-global-dimension characterisation, absent from Mathlib v4.31 (`IsRegularLocalRing` has only `RegularLocalRing/Defs.lean`). At non-closed points (ii.A) correctly fails (`finrank Cotangent = ht z ≠ n`). Do not attempt 00OF as a side quest — it is a large homological build.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.

19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom

279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion

291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by

296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp

299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.

604	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
605	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
606	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
607	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
608	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.

611	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The

666	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
667	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
668	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
669	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
670	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
671	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
672	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),

674	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by

678	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`

680	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`).
681	  haveI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
682	  haveI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
683	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the

685	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).

687	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))

692	  -- [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance gives
693	  -- `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)` (the connected-
694	  -- pullback substrate). The iso construction proceeds by:
695	  -- 1. Build an open immersion `e : pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ ⟶
696	  --    G_K.left` via `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
697	  --    (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ...`, open-immersion status from

699	  --    i₃ = 𝟙 mono).

701	  --    via clopen-+-connected → connected-component bidirectional argument.
702	  -- 3. Apply `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` to get the iso between
703	  --    `pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ` and
704	  --    `(identityComponentCarrier G_K).toScheme = (IdentityComponent G_K).left`.

707	  sorry

712	
713	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
714	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
715	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
716	
717	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
718	`prp:pic0`). -/

720	namespace Scheme
721	
722	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
723	
724	Defined as the identity component
725	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
726	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
727	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
728	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
729	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
730	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
731	base field.
732	
733	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
734	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
735	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
736	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
737	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/

739	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
741	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
742	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
743	  sorry
744	
745	/-! ## §3. The degree map
746	
747	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
748	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
749	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
750	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
751	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
752	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
753	
754	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
755	
756	namespace PicScheme
757	
758	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
759	
760	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
761	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
762	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
763	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
764	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
765	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
766	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
767	relative Picard functor).
768	
769	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
770	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
771	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
772	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
773	
774	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
775	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
776	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
777	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
778	`sorry`. -/

780	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
781	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
782	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
783	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
784	  sorry

789	
790	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
791	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
792	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
793	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
794	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by

797	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
798	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/

800	namespace Pic0Scheme
801	
802	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
803	
804	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
805	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
806	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
807	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
808	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
809	
810	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
811	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
812	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
813

816	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
817	follow-up.
818	
819	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
820	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of

822	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹

824	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
825	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the

828	  identity component).

830	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
831	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
832	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
833	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
834	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧

836	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=

842	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
843	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
844	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
845	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where

847	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
848	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`

852	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
853	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :

875	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :

12	/-!
13	# Geometrically connected schemes
14	
15	In this file we define geometrically connected morphisms of schemes. A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is
16	geometrically connected if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K` a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is connected.
17	In the case where `Y = Spec K` for some field `K` this recovers the standard definition
18	of a geometrically connected scheme over a field.
19	
20	## Main results
21	
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyConnected`: A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically connected if
23	  for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K` a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is connected.
24	- `GeometricallyConnected.iff_geometricallyConnected_fiber`: A scheme is geometrically connected
25	  over `S` iff the fibers of all `s : S` are geometrically connected.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
32	
33	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
36	
37	/-- We say that a morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically connected if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
38	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is connected. -/
39	@[mk_iff]
40	class GeometricallyConnected (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
41	  geometrically_connectedSpace : geometrically (ConnectedSpace ·) f
42	
43	lemma GeometricallyConnected.eq_geometrically :
44	    @GeometricallyConnected = geometrically (ConnectedSpace ·) := by
45	  ext; exact geometricallyConnected_iff _
46	
47	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyConnected :=
48	  GeometricallyConnected.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
49	
50	instance [GeometricallyConnected g] : GeometricallyConnected (pullback.fst f g) :=
51	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
52	
53	instance [GeometricallyConnected f] : GeometricallyConnected (pullback.snd f g) :=
54	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
55	
56	instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyConnected f] : GeometricallyConnected (f ∣_ V) :=
57	  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›
58	
59	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyConnected f] :
60	    GeometricallyConnected (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) :=
61	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance
62	
63	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyConnected f] : ConnectedSpace (f.fiber s) :=
64	  GeometricallyConnected.geometrically_connectedSpace _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
65	
66	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyConnected f] : Surjective f :=
67	  ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨_, (f.range_fiberι x).le ⟨Nonempty.some inferInstance, rfl⟩⟩⟩
68	
69	lemma Scheme.Hom.isConnected_preimage_singleton [GeometricallyConnected f] (x : S) :
70	    _root_.IsConnected (f ⁻¹' {x}) := by
71	  rw [← f.range_fiberι, ← Set.image_univ]
72	  exact isConnected_univ.image _ (f.fiberι _).continuous.continuousOn
73	
74	lemma Scheme.Hom.isConnected_preimage [GeometricallyConnected f] (hf : IsOpenMap f)
75	    {s : Set S} (hs : _root_.IsConnected s) (hs' : IsClosed s) : _root_.IsConnected (f ⁻¹' s) := by
76	  refine Topology.IsCoinducing.isConnected_preimage_of_isClosed f.isConnected_preimage_singleton
77	    ?_ hs' hs
78	  exact (hf.isQuotientMap f.continuous f.surjective).isCoinducing

81	then `f` induces a homeomorphism between the connected components of `X` and `S`. -/

84	def Scheme.Hom.connectedComponentsHomeomorph [GeometricallyConnected f] (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
85	    ConnectedComponents X ≃ₜ ConnectedComponents S :=
86	  (hf.isQuotientMap f.continuous f.surjective).isCoinducing.connectedComponentsHomeomorph
87	    f.isConnected_preimage_singleton

89	lemma GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace [GeometricallyConnected f] [ConnectedSpace S]
90	    (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
91	    ConnectedSpace X := by
92	  simpa [connectedSpace_iff_univ] using f.isConnected_preimage hf isConnected_univ
93	
94	/-- If `X` is geometrically connected over a point, then it is connected. -/
95	lemma GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace_of_subsingleton
96	    [GeometricallyConnected f] [Subsingleton S] [Nonempty S] : ConnectedSpace X :=
97	  have : ConnectedSpace S := ⟨‹_›⟩
98	  GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace (f := f) fun _ _ ↦ isOpen_discrete _
99	
100	instance [GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y] :
101	    ConnectedSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=
102	  GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace (pullback.snd _ _) (pullback.snd f g).isOpenMap
103

12	/-!
13	# Geometrically-`P` schemes over a field
14	
15	In this file we define the basic interface for properties like geometrically reduced,
16	geometrically irreducible, geometrically connected etc. In this file
17	we treat an abstract property of schemes `P` and derive the general properties that are
18	shared by all of these variants.
19	
20	A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
21	morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`.
22	
23	## Main definitions and results
24	
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.geometrically`: The morphism property of geometrically-`P` morphisms
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField`: `f : X ⟶ Y` is
27	  geometrically-`P` if and only if for every `y : Y`, the fiber `f ⁻¹ {y}` is geometrically-`P`
28	  over `Spec κ(y)`.
29	
30	## Notes
31	
32	This contribution was created as part of the Formalising Algebraic Geometry workshop 2025 in
33	Heidelberg.
34	-/
35	
36	@[expose] public section
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits CommRingCat
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
43	
44	/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
45	morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`. -/
46	def geometrically (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u} :=
47	  fun X Y f ↦ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y)
48	    ⦃Z : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Z ⟶ X) (snd : Z ⟶ Spec (.of K)),
49	    IsPullback fst snd f y → P Z
50	
51	lemma geometrically_eq_universally (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
52	    geometrically P = .universally fun X Y _ ↦ IsIntegral Y → Subsingleton Y → P X := by
53	  ext X Y f
54	  refine ⟨fun hf Z W snd q fst h _ _ ↦ ?_, fun hf aK y Z W fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
55	  · let := (isField_of_isIntegral_of_subsingleton W).toField
56	    apply hf (W.isoSpec.inv ≫ q) snd (fst ≫ W.isoSpec.hom)
57	    apply h.flip.of_iso (.refl _) (.refl _) W.isoSpec (.refl _) <;> simp
58	  · exact hf _ _ _ h.flip inferInstance inferInstance
59	
60	lemma geometrically_inf (P Q : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
61	    geometrically (P ⊓ Q) = geometrically P ⊓ geometrically Q := by
62	  simp only [geometrically_eq_universally, ← MorphismProperty.universally_inf]
63	  congr with X Y f
64	  exact ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨(H · · |>.1), (H · · |>.2)⟩, fun H a b ↦ ⟨H.1 a b, H.2 a b⟩⟩
65	
66	variable (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u})
67	
68	instance : (geometrically P).IsStableUnderBaseChange := by
69	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally]
70	  infer_instance
71	
72	instance [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] : IsZariskiLocalAtTarget (geometrically P) := by
73	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally]
74	  refine universally_isZariskiLocalAtTarget _ fun {X} Y f ι U hU H _ _ ↦ ?_
75	  obtain ⟨y⟩ := (inferInstance : Nonempty Y)
76	  obtain ⟨i, hy⟩ := hU.exists_mem y
77	  have heq : U i = ⊤ := eq_top_iff.mpr fun z _ ↦ by rwa [Subsingleton.elim z y]
78	  let e : ↑(U i) ≅ Y := Y.isoOfEq heq ≪≫ Y.topIso
79	  let e' : ↑(f ⁻¹ᵁ U i) ≅ X := X.isoOfEq (by simp [heq]) ≪≫ X.topIso
80	  exact P.prop_of_iso e' <| H i (.of_isIso e.inv) (e.hom.homeomorph.subsingleton_congr.mpr ‹_›)
81	
82	section geometrically
83	
84	variable {P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}} {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ Y}
85	
86	lemma pullback_of_geometrically (hf : geometrically P f) (K : Type u) [Field K]
87	    (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y) : P (Limits.pullback f y) :=
88	  hf _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
89	
90	lemma pullback_of_geometrically' (hf : geometrically P f) (K : Type u) [Field K]
91	    (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y) : P (Limits.pullback y f) :=
92	  hf _ _ _ (.flip <| .of_hasPullback _ _)
93	
94	lemma geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] :
95	    geometrically P f ↔
96	      ∀ (K : Type u) [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y), P (Limits.pullback f y) := by
97	  refine ⟨fun h K _ _ ↦ pullback_of_geometrically h _ _, fun H K _ _ Y fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
98	  exact P.prop_of_iso h.isoPullback.symm (H _ _)
99	
100	lemma fiber_of_geometrically (hf : geometrically P f) (y : Y) : P (f.fiber y) :=
101	  pullback_of_geometrically hf _ _
102	
103	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
104	/-- `P` holds geometrically for `f` if and only if all fibers are geometrically `P`. -/
105	lemma geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField :
106	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ (y : Y), geometrically P (f.fiberToSpecResidueField y) := by
107	  refine ⟨fun hf y ↦ (geometrically P).pullback_snd _ _ hf, fun H ↦ ?_⟩
108	  intro K _ y Z fst snd h
109	  obtain ⟨⟨y, φ⟩, rfl⟩ := (Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField _ _).symm.surjective y
110	  let p : Z ⟶ f.fiber y :=
111	    pullback.lift fst (snd ≫ Spec.map φ) (by simp [h.w, Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField])
112	  apply H y (Spec.map φ) p snd
113	  simp only [Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField, Equiv.coe_fn_symm_mk] at h
114	  refine .flip (.of_bot (.flip ?_) ?_ (IsPullback.of_hasPullback f (Y.fromSpecResidueField y)).flip)
115	  · convert! h
116	    simp [p]
117	  · simp [p, Scheme.Hom.fiberToSpecResidueField]
118	
119	/-- This holds in particular if `Y = Spec K`. -/
120	lemma self_of_isIntegral_of_geometrically [IsIntegral Y] [Subsingleton Y] (hf : geometrically P f) :
121	    P X := by
122	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally] at hf
123	  exact MorphismProperty.universally_le _ _ hf ‹_› ‹_›
124	
125	variable {P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}} {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {f : X ⟶ Spec (.of R)}
126	
127	lemma geometrically_iff_of_commRing :
128	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] [Algebra R K] ⦃Y : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Y ⟶ X)
129	      (snd : Y ⟶ Spec (.of K)), IsPullback fst snd f (Spec.map <| ofHom (algebraMap R K)) →
130	      P Y := by
131	  refine ⟨fun hs K _ _ Z fst snd h ↦ hs _ _ _ h, fun H K _ y Z fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
132	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y
133	  algebraize [φ.hom]
134	  exact H fst snd h
135	
136	lemma geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] :
137	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra R K],
138	      P (Limits.pullback f (Spec.map <| ofHom <| algebraMap R K)) := by
139	  refine ⟨fun hf K _ _ ↦ pullback_of_geometrically hf _ _, fun H ↦ ?_⟩
140	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
141	  intro K _ y
142	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y
143	  algebraize [φ.hom]
144	  exact H K
145	
146	end geometrically
147	
148	end AlgebraicGeometry

public import Mathlib.RingTheory.AlgebraicIndependent.RankAndCardinality

/-!

# Linearly disjoint fields

This file contains basics about the linearly disjoint fields.
We adapt the definitions in <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linearly_disjoint>.
See the file `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/LinearDisjoint.lean`
and `Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean` for details.

## Main definitions

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint`: an intermediate field `A` of `E / F`
  and an abstract field `L` between `E / F`
  (as a special case, two intermediate fields) are linearly disjoint over `F`,
  if they are linearly disjoint as subalgebras (`Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint`).

## Implementation notes

The `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint` is stated for two `Subalgebra`s. The original design of
`IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint` is also stated for two `IntermediateField`s
(see `IntermediateField.linearDisjoint_iff'` for the original statement).
But it's probably useful if one of them can be generalized to an abstract field
(see <https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/9651#discussion_r1464070324>).
This leads to the current design of `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint`
which is for one `IntermediateField` and one abstract field.
It is not generalized to two abstract fields as this will break the dot notation.

## Main results

### Equivalent characterization of linear disjointness

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.linearIndependent_left`:
  if `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint, then any `F`-linearly independent family on `A` remains
  linearly independent over `L`.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_basis_left`:
  conversely, if there exists an `F`-basis of `A` which remains linearly independent over `L`, then
  `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.linearIndependent_right`:
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.linearIndependent_right'`:
  if `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint, then any `F`-linearly independent family on `L` remains
  linearly independent over `A`.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_basis_right`:
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_basis_right'`:
  conversely, if there exists an `F`-basis of `L` which remains linearly independent over `A`, then
  `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.linearIndependent_mul`:
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.linearIndependent_mul'`:
  if `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint, then for any family of
  `F`-linearly independent elements `{ a_i }` of `A`, and any family of
  `F`-linearly independent elements `{ b_j }` of `L`, the family `{ a_i * b_j }` in `S` is
  also `F`-linearly independent.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_basis_mul`:
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_basis_mul'`:
  conversely, if `{ a_i }` is an `F`-basis of `A`, if `{ b_j }` is an `F`-basis of `L`,
  such that the family `{ a_i * b_j }` in `E` is `F`-linearly independent,
  then `A` and `L` are linearly disjoint.

### Equivalent characterization by `IsDomain` or `IsField` of tensor product

The following results are related to the equivalent characterizations in
<https://mathoverflow.net/questions/8324>.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.isDomain'`,
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.exists_field_of_isDomain`:
  if `A` and `B` are field extensions of `F`, then `A ⊗[F] B`
  is a domain if and only if there exists a field extension of `F` that `A` and `B`
  embed into with linearly disjoint images.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.isField_of_forall`,
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_isField'`:
  if `A` and `B` are field extensions of `F`, then `A ⊗[F] B`
  is a field if and only if for any field extension of `F` that `A` and `B` embed into, their
  images are linearly disjoint.

- `Algebra.TensorProduct.isField_of_isAlgebraic`:
  if `E` and `K` are field extensions of `F`, one of them is algebraic, and
  `E ⊗[F] K` is a domain, then `E ⊗[F] K` is also a field.
  See `Algebra.TensorProduct.isAlgebraic_of_isField` for its converse (in an earlier file).

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.isField_of_isAlgebraic`,
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.isField_of_isAlgebraic'`:
  if `A` and `B` are field extensions of `F`, one of them is algebraic, such that they are linearly
  disjoint (more generally, if there exists a field extension of `F` that they embed into with
  linearly disjoint images), then `A ⊗[F] B` is a field.

### Other main results

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.symm`, `IntermediateField.linearDisjoint_comm`:
  linear disjointness is symmetric.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.map`:
  linear disjointness is preserved by algebra homomorphism.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.rank_sup`,
  `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.finrank_sup`:
  if `A` and `B` are linearly disjoint,
  then the rank of `A ⊔ B` is equal to the product of the rank of `A` and `B`.

- `IntermediateField.LinearDisjoint.of_finrank_sup`:
  conversely, if `A` and `B` are finite extensions,
  such that rank of `A ⊔ B` is equal to the product of the rank of `A` and `B`,
  then `A` and `B` are linearly disjoint.

module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Basic
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UniversallyOpen

/-!
# Geometrically Irreducible Schemes

## Main results
- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`:
  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible.
  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by `infer_instance`)
- `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`:
  A scheme is geometrically irreducible over `S` iff the fibers of all
  `s : S` are geometrically irreducible.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`:
  If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open (e.g. when flat + finite presentation),
  over an irreducible scheme, then `X` is also irreducible.
  In particular, the base change of a geometrically irreducible and universally open scheme to an
  irreducible scheme is irreducible (by `infer_instance`).
-/

universe u

@[expose] public section

open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)

/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible. -/
@[mk_iff]
class GeometricallyIrreducible (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  geometrically_irreducibleSpace : geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f

lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically :
    @GeometricallyIrreducible = geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) := by
  ext; exact geometricallyIrreducible_iff _

instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible :=
  GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance

instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.fst f g) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance

instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.snd f g) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance

instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (f ∣_ V) :=
  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›

module

public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Basis.VectorSpace
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.FaithfullyFlat.Basic
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.FractionRing
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.Hom.InjSurj

/-!

# Nontriviality of tensor product of algebras

This file contains some more results on nontriviality of tensor product of algebras.

-/

public section

open TensorProduct

namespace Algebra.TensorProduct

/-- If `A`, `B` are `R`-algebras, `R` injects into `A` and `B`, and `A` and `B` are domains
(which implies `R` is also a domain), then `A ⊗[R] B` is nontrivial. -/
theorem nontrivial_of_algebraMap_injective_of_isDomain
    (R A B : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing B] [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B]
    (ha : Function.Injective (algebraMap R A)) (hb : Function.Injective (algebraMap R B))
    [IsDomain A] [IsDomain B] : Nontrivial (A ⊗[R] B) := by
  haveI := ha.isDomain _
  let FR := FractionRing R
  let FA := FractionRing A
  let FB := FractionRing B
  let fa : FR →ₐ[R] FA := IsFractionRing.liftAlgHom (g := Algebra.ofId R FA)
    ((IsFractionRing.injective A FA).comp ha)
  let fb : FR →ₐ[R] FB := IsFractionRing.liftAlgHom (g := Algebra.ofId R FB)
    ((IsFractionRing.injective B FB).comp hb)
  algebraize_only [fa.toRingHom, fb.toRingHom]
  letI : CompatibleSMul FR R FA FB := CompatibleSMul.isScalarTower
  exact Algebra.TensorProduct.mapOfCompatibleSMul FR R R FA FB |>.comp
    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R A FA) (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R B FB))
    |>.toRingHom.domain_nontrivial

end Algebra.TensorProduct
